manifest.name: Tooltip
manifest.description: A small, non-interactive pop-up that displays informative text when a user hovers over or focuses on an element.

minimal.title: Default
minimal.code:
(

This is a tooltip

 )

guidance.whenToUse: Use a Tooltip to display a small amount of non-essential information or context related to an element when a user hovers over it or focuses on it via keyboard. Ideal for explaining icons, abbreviations, or providing quick labels for interactive elements.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Do NOT use a Tooltip for critical information that requires user interaction or immediate attention. For longer content, interactive elements, or crucial details, consider a Popover or Dialog. Avoid using tooltips for decorative elements or when the content is already clear from context.
guidance.accessibility: Ensure tooltips are accessible to keyboard and screen reader users. The content should be available via 'aria-describedby' or similar mechanisms. Tooltips should appear on keyboard focus and disappear on blur. Users should be able to dismiss tooltips without needing to interact with the triggering element again. Radix UI's Tooltip component handles many of these accessibility concerns automatically.

manifest.semanticDescription: The Tooltip component provides a brief, non-interactive textual label that appears on hover or focus of its trigger element. It's designed to enhance user understanding by offering supplementary information without obstructing the primary content. Key features include automatic positioning, accessible keyboard interaction, and configurable delay for appearance and disappearance. Use cases include clarifying ambiguous icons, providing full labels for truncated text, explaining input fields, or giving additional context to interactive controls without cluttering the UI.
